grounding_note: Expansion batch: more judgment-existence and debtor-bankruptcy disclosures on the creditor side.

scenarios.opening_query: My old roommate skipped out owing me four months of his share of rent and utilities.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I already won in small claims — $3,900 judgment — and just found out where he works; I need it collected.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I already won $3,900 in small claims — and I just found his employer. I need it collected.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a judgment exists to enforce
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I haven't filed anything — I don't even know if suing is worth it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I haven't filed anything yet — not sure it's even worth suing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Judgment-plus-located-employer is a pure enforcement posture.

scenarios.opening_query: The company that owes my consulting invoice keeps promising 'next quarter' while I fall behind on my own bills.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Their 'next quarter' became a Chapter 11 filing yesterday — I'm unsecured for $28,000 and there's a claims-bar date coming.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They filed Chapter 11 yesterday — I'm unsecured for $28,000 with a claims-bar date coming.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the debtor company filed bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No filing — they're solvent and paying newer vendors first; I need pressure applied.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No filing — they're solvent, just paying newer vendors first.
scenarios.vetting_note: Bar-date urgency is what makes this disclosure decisive.

scenarios.opening_query: I loaned a friend money against his boat and he's stopped answering my calls entirely.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor) || Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's no paperwork at all — a handshake loan between friends, and he claims it was 'help,' not a loan. It's a private dispute about what the deal even was.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's no paperwork — a handshake between friends, and now he calls it 'help,' not a loan.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether documentation supports a secured-creditor posture
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I have a signed promissory note with the boat as collateral, properly notarized — I want to enforce against the boat.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I have a notarized note with the boat as collateral — I want to enforce against the boat.
scenarios.vetting_note: Documentation existence separates creditor enforcement from he-said private dispute.

scenarios.opening_query: Our HOA can't collect dues from one owner and it's forcing the rest of us to cover the gap.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Homeowners Assoc || Bankruptcy > Creditors || Debtor/Creditor > General (Creditor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: He filed Chapter 13 — the board got the notice, and our lien and the arrears are now tangled in his plan; the association needs bankruptcy counsel.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He filed Chapter 13 — our lien and the arrears are caught in his plan, and the board needs bankruptcy counsel.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the delinquent owner is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No bankruptcy — the question is our own lien and foreclosure powers under the CC&Rs.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No bankruptcy — it's about our lien and foreclosure powers under the CC&Rs.
scenarios.vetting_note: Board-side intake; the filing reroutes association collection into bankruptcy practice.

scenarios.opening_query: A customer's check bounced for a big landscaping job and now he's dodging me while enjoying the new yard.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DA's bad-check unit took the case — he wrote it on a closed account, and they're prosecuting; I mostly need to understand restitution versus suing him myself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DA's bad-check unit is prosecuting — closed account. I need to understand restitution versus suing myself.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal bad-check prosecution is underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The DA declined — insufficient funds isn't enough for them, so it's civil collection or nothing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The DA declined — so it's civil collection or nothing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Prosecution status determines whether restitution rides the criminal case.

scenarios.opening_query: We won at trial against our old contractor but he transferred everything to his wife's name.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Judgment Collection || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The transfers happened the week after the verdict — our lawyer says that's a fraudulent-conveyance lawsuit against both of them, a new case, not just collection paperwork.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He moved it all the week after the verdict — we're told that's a fraudulent-conveyance suit against both of them, a whole new case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether post-judgment transfers require a fraudulent-conveyance action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'transfers' were old — years before our case — so it's ordinary garnishment and lien work against what he still has.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Turns out the transfers were years before our case — so it's ordinary garnishment and liens against what's left.
scenarios.vetting_note: Transfer timing separates routine enforcement from a fraudulent-transfer suit.
